source_text: soldiers then carried On the breast of the vessel bright-shining jewels, Handsome war-armor;

subject: sailing-men
source_text: Till twenty and four hours thereafter The twist-stemmed vessel had traveled such distance That the sailing-men saw the sloping embankments, The sea cliffs gleaming, precipitous mountains, Nesses enormous: they were nearing the limits At the end of the ocean. {In twenty four hours they reach the shores of Hrothgar's dominions}

subject: war-troop
source_text: More boldly never have warriors ventured Hither to come;

subject: mail-covered warriors
source_text: And hither 'neath helmets have hied o'er the ocean?

subject: Lord of the Weders
source_text: My father was known, a noble head-warrior Ecgtheow titled;

source_text: till the curved-neckèd bark shall Waft back again the well-beloved hero O'er the way of the water to Weder dominions.

source_text: Higelac's mates are we; Beowulf hight I.

subject: Lord of the Weders
source_text: His armor seamed over by the art of the craftsman):

source_text: To West-Dane warriors, I ween, for to render 'Gainst Grendel's grimness gracious assistance:

source_text: the journey[1] of Beowulf, Sea-farer doughty, gave sorrow to Unferth

source_text: A grim grappling, if Grendel thou darest For the space of a night near-by to wait for!"

source_text: that we yet would adventure Out on the ocean; it all we accomplished.

source_text: He the home and manor of Hrothgar had sought:

subject: Grendel opens the door
source_text: The fell one had flung then--his fury so bitter-- Open the entrance. Early thereafter The foeman trod the shining hall-pavement,

subject: Long-hating foeman
source_text: {Grendel immediately seizes a sleeping warrior, and devours him.} But on earliest occasion he quickly laid hold of A soldier asleep, suddenly tore him, Bit his bone-prison, the blood drank in currents, Swallowed in mouthfuls: he soon had the dead man's Feet and hands, too, eaten entirely.

subject: Long-hating foeman
source_text: Would fly to his covert, seek the devils' assembly:

source_text: Angry they both were, Archwarders raging. ... where the grim ones did struggle.

source_text: Who heard from the wall the weeping and plaining

source_text: But Higelac's hardy henchman and kinsman Held him by the hand;

subject: Final struggle with Grendel
source_text: A body-wound suffered The direful demon, damage incurable Was seen on his shoulder, his sinews were shivered, His body did burst.

subject: War

source_text: Early she found then who fifty of winters The course of the currents kept in her fury, Grisly and greedy, that the grim one's dominion Some one of men from above was exploring.

source_text: But many a mere-beast tormented him swimming, Flood-beasts no few with fierce-biting tusks did Break through his burnie, the brave one pursued they.

source_text: Was Higelac's kinsman

source_text: Hopeless of living, hotly he smote her, That the fiend-woman's neck firmly it grappled,

subject: Lord of the Weders
source_text: And turned by the wall then, Higelac's vassal

source_text: And he cut off his head then.

subject: he

source_text: From the ness-edge departed The bold-mooded Scyldings

subject: weapon

source_text: Fared from far-lands, we're firmly determined To seek King Higelac.

source_text: And life thou yet holdest, the Sea-Geats will never Find a more fitting folk-lord to choose them, Gem-ward of heroes, than thou mightest prove thee, If the kingdom of kinsmen thou carest to govern.

source_text: A glove was suspended Spacious and wondrous, in art-fetters fastened... He down in its depths would do me unsadly One among many, deed-doer raging,

source_text: But sturdy of strength he strove to outdo me, Hand-ready grappled.

source_text: oft light-ringing harp-strings, Joy-telling wood, were touched by the brave one;

subject: son of King Healfdene
source_text: The age-hoary warrior afterward began to Mourn for the might that marked him in youth-days;

subject: banquet
source_text: Yet his right-hand guarded his footstep in Heorot... When daylight had dawned, and down to the banquet We had sat us together.

subject: Æschere
source_text: And with mighty mainstrength murdered a hero. {Æschere falls a prey to her vengeance.} There the spirit of Æschere, agèd adviser, Was ready to vanish;

subject: son of King Healfdene
source_text: By the life of thee the land-prince then me Besought very sadly, in sea-currents' eddies To display my prowess, to peril my safety, Might-deeds accomplish;

subject: Lord of the Weders
source_text: But few of my folk-kin find I surviving But thee, dear Higelac!

source_text: said King Heregar owned it, Dane-prince for long

source_text: Wonder-gem rare that Wealhtheow gave him

source_text: I am told that to Hygd he proffered the necklace, Wonder-gem rare... soon did the jewel Embellish her bosom, when the beer-feast was over.

subject: Lord of the Weders
source_text: In Beowulf's keeping he placed it and gave him Seven of thousands, manor and lordship.

source_text: The beat-plated beaker he bare to his chieftain

source_text: He no longer would wait on the wall, but departed Fire-impelled, flaming.

subject: discovery of the gold
source_text: the loathèd one wished to Pay for the dear-valued drink-cup with fire. Then the day was done as the dragon would have it, He no longer would wait on the wall, but departed Fire-impelled, flaming.

source_text: He no longer would wait on the wall, but departed

source_text: while this weapon endureth That late and early often did serve me

source_text: Fame-deeds perform, folk-warden prudent,

source_text: the ring-twisted creature's Heart was then ready to seek for a struggle.

source_text: he saw his dear liegelord Enduring the heat 'neath helmet and visor.

source_text: This the dragon discovered on their coming together.

source_text: Let us wend us towards him, the war-prince to succor, While the heat yet rageth, horrible fire-fight.

subject: Wiglaf
source_text: Went under helmet to the help of his chieftain,

subject: Lord of the Weders
source_text: The fight-armor failed then to furnish assistance To the youthful spear-hero:

subject: Lord of the Weders
source_text: Then the warrior-king was careful of glory, He soundly smote with sword-for-the-battle, That it stood in the head by hatred driven;

subject: Dragon's third onset
source_text: Charged on the strong one, when chance was afforded, Heated and war-grim, seized on his neck With teeth that were bitter;

subject: Lord of the Weders
source_text: They had felled the enemy (life drove out then Puissant prowess), the pair had destroyed him, Land-chiefs related:

subject: Wiglaf
source_text: So he struck the strife-bringing strange-comer lower, Earl-thane in armor, that in went the weapon Gleaming and plated,

subject: the worm
object: being put to sleep with the sword
source_text: now the worm is a-lying, Sore-wounded sleepeth, disseized of his treasure.

subject: Lord of the Weders
source_text: He felled the foe (life drove out strength), and they then both had destroyed him, chieftains related.

subject: Wiglaf
source_text: went in his armor, His well-woven ring-mail, 'neath the roof of the barrow.

subject: Wiglaf plunders the dragon's den
source_text: WIGLAF PLUNDERS THE DRAGON'S DEN... Wonder on the wall, and the worm-creature's cavern

subject: Wiglaf
source_text: He thereupon 'gan to lave him with water,

subject: The final battle
source_text: The flier-from-farland fell to the earth Hard by his hoard-house... On the barrow abiding.

subject: Wiglaf
source_text: Wiglaf is sorely grieved to see his lord look so un-warlike.

subject: The ten thanes
source_text: The tardy-at-battle returned from the thicket, The timid truce-breakers ten all together,

subject: liegelord
source_text: That he wildly wasted his war-gear undoubtedly When battle o'ertook him.

subject: Wulfgar
source_text: Little reserved he Of news that was known, who the ness-cliff did travel,

subject: Frankmen
source_text: where the Frankmen in battle Humbled him

subject: Battle in Friesland
source_text: That the mail-clad warrior must sink in the battle, Fell 'mid his folk-troop

subject: Higelac
source_text: where the Frankmen in battle Humbled him

subject: Geatmen
source_text: When for pride overweening the War-Scylfings first did Seek the Geatmen with savage intentions.

subject: Geatmen
source_text: Reaved of their ruler, they Ravenswood entered.

subject: Assault on the Geatmen
source_text: Reaved of their ruler, they Ravenswood entered. Then with vast-numbered forces he assaulted the remnant

subject: Ongentheow
source_text: Then with vast-numbered forces he assaulted the remnant

subject: Ongentheow
source_text: Then with edges of irons was Ongentheow driven, The gray-haired to tarry, that the troop-ruler had to Suffer the power solely of Eofor:

subject: son of Wonred
source_text: But his head-shielding helmet first hewed he to pieces,

subject: Hewing of Ongentheow's helmet
source_text: But his head-shielding helmet first hewed he to pieces, That flecked with gore perforce he did totter, Fell to the earth;

subject: son of Wonred
source_text: This pending, one hero plundered the other, His armor of iron from Ongentheow ravished, His hard-sword hilted and helmet together;

subject: Ongentheow
source_text: His armor of iron from Ongentheow ravished,

subject: son of King Healfdene
source_text: After the downfall of Hrothgar's family, Beowulf was king of the Danes, or Scyldings.

subject: Lord of the Weders
source_text: When the strength-famous trooper has attained to the end of Life-days allotted, then no longer the man may Remain with his kinsmen where mead-cups are flowing. So to Beowulf happened when the ward of the barrow, Assaults, he sought for: himself had no knowledge How his leaving this life was likely to happen.

subject: henchmen
source_text: 'neath the enemy's roof he Went with the seven;

subject: hero-earls
object: pushing the dragon over the wall
source_text: the dragon eke pushed they, The worm o'er the wall

subject: Wiglaf
object: laying hold of the heathenish hoard
source_text: Hastily seized I And held in my hands a huge-weighing burden Of hoard-treasures costly, hither out bare them To my liegelord belovèd:

subject: wailing war-heroes
source_text: Then wailing war-heroes their world-famous chieftain, Their liegelord beloved, laid in the middle.

subject: Lord of the Weders
source_text: Heathoræmas reaches Breca in the swimming-match with Beowulf.

subject: Professor Michael S. Hart
source_text: Professor Michael S. Hart was the originator of the Project Gutenberg concept of a library of electronic works that could be freely shared with anyone. For forty years, he produced and distributed Project Gutenberg eBooks with only a loose network of volunteer support.
